message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): chunked transfer-encoding response contract (tick 402)

Added `distributed_http_client_parses_chunked_transfer_encoding` —
pins that `DistributedHttpClient::get` correctly handles a response
encoded with `Transfer-Encoding: chunked`.

Why: real HTTP/1.1 servers use chunked encoding for responses whose
length isn't known up-front (SSE streams, streaming APIs, dynamically
generated content, compression pipelines). A client that assumed
Content-Length always exists would fail to parse or hang waiting for
EOF instead of parsing chunk framing.

Test server sends a valid chunked response with body "HelloWorld"
split into 3 chunks (5+3+2 bytes) + 0-size terminator:

    HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\n
    Transfer-Encoding: chunked\r\n
    \r\n
    5\r\nHello\r\n
    3\r\nWor\r\n
    2\r\nld\r\n
    0\r\n\r\n

Client must reassemble the chunks as b"HelloWorld" (10 bytes, with
the size prefixes and CRLF separators stripped).

Pairs with tick 319's Content-Length-bounded response test —
together they cover both response-body-framing strategies allowed
by HTTP/1.1.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 23s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
